reasoning: The claim that Israel used drones playing recordings of crying children or distressed women to lure Palestinians initially emerged in April 2024 from Palestinian sources and Euro-Med Human Rights Monitor, and circulated widely on social media. Despite this visibility, extensive searches confirm that no major West-aligned news outlet (such as CNN, BBC, Reuters, AP, The New York Times, Washington Post, or The Guardian) has run with this story as credible over the subsequent two years. The fact-checking site Snopes investigated the claim in April 2024 and found it rested on a single unverified video and very few eyewitnesses. Notably, The Washington Post reported in December 2023 that the IDF allegedly found Hamas booby traps utilizing speakers playing children's crying sounds to lure Israeli soldiers. This earlier reciprocal accusation increases the evidentiary burden for Western media outlets, who are likely extremely cautious about attributing a similar psychological warfare tactic to the IDF without rock-solid proof. Because the resolution criteria require a major West-aligned outlet to run the story without substantial criticism before the conflict concludes (plus 3 months), and given that it has been two years since the rumors peaked without any such corroboration, the probability remains low and in line with the market consensus of approximately 10%.
